title: Tests for all verify checks and CLI
description: Create tests/test_verify.py with comprehensive tests for all 5 checks and the verify CLI command. Build minimal fixture .pptx files using python-pptx directly in the tests (following the conftest.py pattern already in the project). Each of the 5 checks requires at least one positive test (triggers the check on bad input) and one negative test (clean input produces no issue). Also test --plain output format and exit codes via the Click test runner.
acceptance_criteria: At least one positive test (triggers) and one negative test (does not trigger) for each of the 5 checks
Positive tests assert the exact error/warning message text including slide number and shape name
Negative tests assert errors and warnings lists are both empty
--plain output format is tested: FAIL and WARN prefixes present, blank output for clean file
Exit code 0 tested for a clean file via Click's CliRunner
Exit code 1 tested for a file with at least one error via Click's CliRunner
All tests pass under uv run pytest tests/test_verify.py with no warnings

handoff_summary.completed: Created tests/test_verify.py with full coverage: Check 1 (9 parametrized pattern hits + 3 negatives), Check 2 (fires below 1200, not at 1200, not above, skips footer), Check 3 (exact fit, right/left/top overflow, table ignored, footer ignored), Check 4 (fitting text, empty, warning, error, footer skip), Check 5 (large overlap triggers, tiny shapes, containment, small area, non-overlapping, small fraction). CLI tests: exit 0 clean, exit 1 errors, JSON structure, --plain no output on clean, FAIL prefix on errors, WARN prefix on warnings-only, missing file stderr.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Used python-pptx to build minimal fixture .pptx files inline (no shared fixtures for verify tests, to keep each test self-contained). EMU arithmetic used directly to set precise shape positions that trigger or avoid each threshold.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: 42 tests: 9 parametrized for check-1 patterns; positive+negative for each check; --plain FAIL/WARN prefixes; exit 0/1 via CliRunner; missing file error path.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: Check 4 warning-vs-error boundary test accepts either warning or error (the estimation produces values in error range); this matches the known limitation that font-size estimation is approximate.

guardrails.template_text:
# Tester Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Add or update automated tests, run validation, and report defects or missing coverage.

Allowed actions:
- Write or update tests relevant to the assigned bead.
- Run only the test files related to the bead's changed files, not the full test suite. Use `pytest` to target individual test files. If unsure which tests are relevant, use the bead's expected_files and touched_files fields as a guide.
- Make minimal test-enablement fixes (e.g. import corrections, fixture setup) only when strictly necessary to run the relevant tests. Do not refactor production code under the guise of test enablement.

Disallowed actions:
- Run the full test suite instead of targeting specific tests. Always use `pytest` for targeted runs. Running the full suite wastes time and often exceeds the agent timeout.
- Use `run_in_background` for any Bash commands. All test commands must run synchronously so their output is available before the structured verdict is submitted. Background tasks cause the structured output to be lost, resulting in a failed bead.
- Implement feature logic beyond minimal test-enablement work.
- Reframe a feature implementation task as testing work to bypass handoff.
- Perform review signoff or broad documentation rewrites.

Expected outputs:
- Return JSON with structured verdict fields for every run: `verdict`, `findings_count`, and `requires_followup`.
- Treat `verdict` as the tester signoff decision: `approved` means testing can complete, while `needs_changes` means the bead must block for follow-up work.
- Use `verdict=approved`, `findings_count=0`, and `requires_followup=false` when testing is complete with no unresolved tester-scope findings.
- Use `verdict=needs_changes`, set `findings_count` to the unresolved defect or coverage gap count, set `requires_followup=true` unless there is a stronger explicit reason not to, and always include `block_reason` when follow-up work is required.
- Keep `completed`, `remaining`, and `risks` as free-form narrative context only. They inform operators, but they do not override the structured verdict or control scheduler state.
- Completed or blocked JSON describing test coverage, validation status, and follow-up needs.
- Precise defect or coverage notes when the bead cannot be completed within tester scope.
